chapter: Chapter 3: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables

questions.question: Explain why a pair of linear equations cannot have exactly two solutions.
questions.answer: Because two straight lines can intersect at only one point.

questions.question: Explain why a dependent system is always consistent.
questions.answer: Because it has infinitely many solutions.

questions.question: Why is graphical method not reliable for exact solutions?
questions.answer: Because solutions depend on scale and drawing accuracy.

questions.question: Explain why algebraic methods are preferred over graphical method.
questions.answer: Because algebraic methods give exact solutions.

questions.question: Why do proportional equations represent the same line?
questions.answer: Because one equation is a multiple of the other.

questions.question: Why does same slope imply parallel lines?
questions.answer: Because both lines rise or fall at the same rate.

questions.question: Why does different slope ensure intersection?
questions.answer: Because lines with different slopes must cross.

questions.question: Why is an inconsistent system not useful in real-life problems?
questions.answer: Because it gives no valid solution.

questions.question: Explain why cross-multiplication fails for parallel lines.
questions.answer: Because the determinant becomes zero.

questions.question: Why must equations be solved simultaneously?
questions.answer: Because the solution must satisfy both equations.

questions.question: Explain why two equations are needed to find two unknowns.
questions.answer: Because one equation cannot determine both unknowns uniquely.

questions.question: Why does the intersection point represent equality of equations?
questions.answer: Because both equations have the same values at that point.

questions.question: Explain why linear equations model uniform change.
questions.answer: Because the rate of change remains constant.

questions.question: Why is an independent system important in applications?
questions.answer: Because it gives a unique and definite solution.

questions.question: Why does elimination method require equal coefficients?
questions.answer: To remove one variable easily by addition or subtraction.

questions.question: Explain why substitution method is easy when coefficient is one.
questions.answer: Because the variable can be isolated easily.

questions.question: Why does graphical intersection indicate solution?
questions.answer: Because both equations are satisfied at that point.

questions.question: Why does a linear graph always form a straight line?
questions.answer: Because the highest power of the variable is one.

questions.question: Explain why linear equations are widely used in daily life.
questions.answer: Because many situations involve constant rates of change.

questions.question: Why is the concept of slope important in solving equations?
questions.answer: Because slope determines the direction and position of a line.

questions.question: Why do coincident lines represent dependent equations?
questions.answer: Because one equation depends on the other.

questions.question: Explain why parallel lines represent inconsistent equations.
questions.answer: Because they never intersect and have no common solution.

questions.question: Why is cross-multiplication method considered fast?
questions.answer: Because it directly gives values without lengthy steps.

questions.question: Why is graphical method useful despite its limitations?
questions.answer: Because it helps visualize the nature of solutions.

questions.question: Explain the importance of solving pair of linear equations.
questions.answer: Because they help solve real-life problems involving two unknowns.
